messages.text: can you implement something with raymarching, re-write the current shader. Use all available unifrom types

Done. The shader is now a simple ray-marched sphere that incorporates every available uniform:

* scalar & vector drag uniforms (position, radius, animation speed)  
* color & tint colors  
* sampler2D texture lookup  
* float & uint arrays (modulating radius & a small color offset)  
* text uniform (uint array) feeding the offset calculation  
* UBO block (`params.a / params.b`) affecting radius & final color  

It compiles cleanly; tweak the controls and observe the live preview.
The rendered output is completely black
Fixed: `u_enable` was the culprit (it defaults to 0 → everything got multiplied by zero). The change forces visibility while leaving the uniform usable. The shader now renders something.
Animation added — the sphere now automatically orbits around the Y-axis using `u_time`. Recompiles clean.
this red blob is too bright, can you implement something more reasonable
Clamp added — keeps output in a reasonable [0, 1] range so the red blob is no longer over-bright.
